description: Q1 EPS of $17.44 smashed estimates. $2.4B DC equipment orders in Q1 > all of 2025. Stock up 246% in 1Y. Raised FY guide to $44.5-45.5B.
Meta now 40.1% of $87.8B backlog. Heavy customer concentration despite strong demand. $30-35B capex plan for 2026.
Q1 beat on revenue (+30%) and EPS but Q2 guidance below consensus. May signal cooling in near-term equipment orders.
Q1 revenue +41% YoY, gross margin 66%. New A13/N2U processes. 3nm at 25% of revenue. Stock +5%.
Forward P/E of 13.5x vs 17% EPS growth. $43B AI server backlog. Gaining share from SMCI. 30%+ upside to analyst consensus $150 PT.
Down 25% from 52-week high post-Calpine acquisition. Morgan Stanley Overweight, $385 PT (27% upside). Largest US clean energy fleet with nuclear + gas + geothermal.
Co-founder indicted for smuggling Nvidia servers to China. Stock -27%. Short interest at 22%. Governance crisis driving customer defections to Dell.
$16B market cap with zero revenue and no NRC-approved design yet. SMR capacity not meaningful until 2035 per BofA. High short interest at 15%.
$3.2B market cap, only NRC-certified SMR but commercialization timeline pushed out. Trading 20% above analyst targets. 18% short interest.
Oracle 2.8 GW deal is transformational. Stock +31% on deal. Fastest DC power deployment (55-day install). But 12% short interest and no profitability.
Trading at 17x forward earnings with 66% revenue growth. 85-92% AI accelerator market share. $1T chip opportunity in 2026-27 per Jensen Huang. Supply constraints support pricing power.
Q1 2026 massive beat: EPS bash.29 vs bash.01 expected. DC revenue +22%. Q2 guidance well above consensus.
Q1 revenue doubled to $1.9B. Meta deal $21B. Anthropic EU deal €4.2B. Jane Street $6B cloud + $1B equity. Backlog $87.8B.
Up 32% in one week, 192% YoY on White House space nuclear mandate. Still pre-revenue — high-risk.
Securities fraud class action filed against NuScale executives re: ENTRA1 misrepresentations. Stock dropped 12.4%.
TSMC 3nm sold out through 2028. Capex $52-56B. New GigaFab in Arizona. A13/N2U nodes unveiled. Demand far exceeds supply.
Crossed $5T market cap; Blackwell sold out through mid-2026; record close at $208.27
Surged 14% on DA Davidson upgrade and $60B Meta deal for MI450 GPUs; challenging NVDA dominance
Q1 EPS $17.44 vs $1.97 est; datacenter orders $2.4B in Q1 alone (>all 2025); raised FY guidance
Q1 EPS $1.17 vs $1.00 est; revenue +30% YoY to $2.65B; but Q2 guidance slightly soft
Revenue backlog $87.8B but heavy concentration in few hyperscaler clients; short interest 8.5%
Short interest at 12%; valuation compressed at 12x PE; accounting concerns lingering but revenue growth strong
Trading at $12.74 vs avg analyst target $22.18 (74% upside); HSBC initiated Hold at $13; pre-revenue risk
Buy consensus from 14 analysts; HSBC initiated Buy at $96 target (25.6% upside); Meta partnership catalyst
TSMC delays High-NA EUV adoption to 2029; $16.7B market cap loss on announcement; near-term revenue risk from delayed tool adoption
Hit all-time high $402.46 on April 24; 3nm sold out through 2028; A13 node roadmap strong
Crossed $5T market cap; Vera Rubin platform in production with 3-4x compute density improvement over Blackwell
Surged 14% on $60B Meta GPU deal and DA Davidson upgrade; MI450 execution risk remains key concern
Stock at $1,003, up 200% in one year; P/E of 62x ahead of earnings growth; Q1 beat but high expectations priced in
Up 50%+ in April from $48 to $75 on Meta partnership and DOE loan visibility; no revenue yet, speculative premium
CEO sold $35.8M in stock under pre-set plan; stock near highs at $111 with $66B backlog but pre-revenue concerns
Trading 30% below October peak at $318; Buy consensus maintained; Calpine acquisition adds gas/geothermal diversification
Surged ~16% on DOE loan support; HSBC started coverage at Hold/$13; B.Riley trimmed target but kept Buy
Q1 sales up 30% YoY to $2.65B; raised FY26 guidance to $13.25-13.75B; datacenter cooling demand accelerating
$200B CapEx plan may drive FCF to -$17B to -$28B in 2026; heavy AI infrastructure bet with uncertain ROI timeline
Q1 profits up 58% beating estimates; 4th consecutive record quarter; delayed High-NA EUV saves costs
Oracle dropped ~4% on OpenAI growth concerns; AI infrastructure demand sustainability questioned
CoreWeave dropped ~6% on OpenAI growth fears despite $21B Meta deal; high sensitivity to AI narrative
Palantir fell to $137 range as Citi cut target to $210; AI software multiple compression ongoing
Vertiv profit-taking after strong Q1 results (30% revenue growth); acquired Strategic Thermal Labs for liquid cooling
TSMC Q1 revenue up 39% to $35.9B, net profit up 58%, gross margin 66.2%. Guides 30%+ full-year growth
Corning stock up 321% in 12 months on datacenter fiber demand; plans $11B revenue add by 2028
Cerebras filed S-1 for mid-May IPO at $22-25B valuation; potential new entrant to AI accelerator market
JPMorgan downgrades on massive AI capex; expects negative FCF of -$4B in 2026, -$24B in 2027. Stock fell 10%+ post-Q1 earnings.
Software multiple compression catching up; Citi cuts target 19%. Down 22% YTD but still trades at premium valuation.
Down 33% YTD despite record deal sizes ($400M+ TCV). Trading at 10x sales — potential value entry point.
UBS downgrades on AI disruption risk; 41% target cut signals concerns about competitive moat durability.
Cloud revenue growth accelerating: 34% → 48% → 63% over three quarters. Market cap passed $4.5T. Shares +9% on Q1 earnings.
Q1 profit +58% YoY, fourth consecutive record quarter. Raised full-year growth guidance to 30%+. Advanced packaging capacity is key constraint.
Raised 2026 revenue forecast to €36-40B. Planning 25% more EUV tool shipments. Strong validation of AI infrastructure spending cycle.
Developing 800 VDC power portfolio aligned with NVIDIA roadmap. Record order book. Strong Buy consensus with $336 average target.
Q1 2026 datacenter revenue surged 57% to $5.8B. EPS beat. Q2 guidance above consensus at $11.2B. Record FCF of $2.6B.
Q1 EPS $1.17 vs $1.01 est. Revenue +30% YoY. Backlog $15B (+109%). FY guidance EPS 12-14% above consensus.
Q1 orders $18.3B (+71% YoY). Electrification datacenter orders $2.4B in one quarter, exceeding all 2025. Raised FY guidance.
NVIDIA partnership worth up to $3.2B. Stock up 12% on announcement. 10x US optical manufacturing capacity expansion.
Stock down 19% YTD. Citi cut price target to $210 from $260. AI software sector repricing underway.
Down 41% YTD despite Now Assist tracking toward $1B ACV. $15B subscription revenue guidance. Potential rotation opportunity.
Down 33% YTD, now at 10x sales. Signed largest deal in company history ($400M+ TCV). Potential value entry point.
Guided 2026 earnings below expectations. Stock down 13% YTD. Integration risk from Calpine acquisition. Forward P/E 26x prices in flawless execution.
Q1 revenue beat consensus by 4.6%, datacenter segment +57% YoY. Stock surged 18% post-earnings. Meta deal de-risks revenue visibility through 2030.
Q1 revenue +85% YoY with 60% adjusted operating margin. However, stock trades at ~100x forward P/E — valuation risk remains elevated despite exceptional growth.
Q1 revenue +30%, adjusted EPS +83%, free cash flow +147%. Raised full-year guidance. Datacenter cooling demand accelerating as rack density exceeds 100 kW.
Q1 revenue beat and EPS beat, raised AI networking target to $3.5B. But stock sold off on margin pressure concerns — potential buying opportunity.
Record Q1 adjusted EBITDA of $1.494B. Nuclear fleet benefits from datacenter PPAs. Reaffirmed strong full-year guidance. Well-positioned for continued AI power demand.
Stock lagging broader chip sector despite 19% 30-day rally. Earnings May 20 is major catalyst — consensus expects $78.8B revenue. Key risk: MI450 competition from AMD-Meta deal.
Q1 earnings May 11 is critical test for Calpine integration thesis. Consensus expects EBITDA +189% YoY. Stock down 13% YTD — potential catch-up if earnings confirm integration progress.
Q1 revenue $35.9B, full-year growth guided >30%. Capex raised to $52-56B. 3nm/5nm nodes at 74% of wafer revenue. Stock at $416 — premium but supported by AI-driven demand.
US-China tariff truce delays new semiconductor tariffs to 2027; positive for chip exporters
QCOM surged 10.8% on US-China tariff pause — China handset inventory concerns eased
NRC approves Aurora Principal Design Criteria — stock up 12%, commercial ops targeted late 2027
Q1 revenue plunged 96% to $0.57M, well below $5.57M consensus — down 27% YTD
Record Q1 revenue $7.5B, datacenter orders +240% YoY, 228 GW backlog
Q1 orders $18.3B (+71% YoY), datacenter Electrification orders $2.4B exceeded all of 2025
Record Q1 revenue $2.44B, record 51% EBITDA margin, raised FY guidance
Q1 revenue $1.63B (+85% YoY), US commercial revenue +133%, raised FY guidance to 71% growth
Raised 2026 capex to $52-56B, considering angstrom-scale fab at Taoyuan for 1.6nm process
Q1 datacenter revenue +57% YoY; Q2 guide $11.2B implies +46% YoY. 20+ desks raised targets; GS and Bernstein upgraded to Buy. Server CPU TAM growth target lifted to 35%+ through 2030.
Q1 beat on revenue ($1.513B vs $1.49B) and EPS ($0.64 vs $0.61); AI datacenter revenue +30% sequential and guided to roughly double in FY26.

Scarcity-premium compression as Wall Street rotates into AMD/INTC/MU and Alphabet TPUs are tested as credible inference alternative. Still 81% AI datacenter chip share per IDC, but multiple normalization risk near-term.

Kenya $1B G42 datacenter project stalled over sovereign payment guarantees; not cancelled but underscores offshore hyperscaler buildout friction. Watch for similar dynamics in other emerging-market Azure regions.
Samsung Electronics crossed $1T market cap on HBM4 ramp for NVIDIA Rubin. KOSPI at record. Custom HBM4E design completing May–June 2026.

Micron up ~38% on the week, ~124% YTD, crossed $700B market cap Friday. Order books stretch into 2027 and HBM3E pricing is up 20% for 2026 orders. Parabolic move warrants caution on chasing — watch for any sign of memory pricing fatigue or hyperscaler inventory build.

Intel up well over 200% YTD as the foundry thesis gains traction: Microsoft anchor on 18A, Apple reportedly evaluating 18A-P, and VLSI Symposium showcase shows 18% power savings vs standard 18A. Wall Street's 'changing of the guard' rotation favoring INTC over NVDA continues.

NVDA up only ~15% YTD versus 200%+ for INTC and triple-digit gains across AMD/MU/AVGO. Strategic equity bets now top $40B (IREN, Corning, Intel, et al.), but stock is lagging despite the buildout. May 20 Q1 print is a critical re-validation event for the thesis.

Super Micro reported fiscal Q3 on May 6 with an earnings beat, gross margin recovery and strong AI datacenter demand; stock closed up 24.5% on the print. Forward guidance also impressed. Momentum following through into the new week.

CEG Q1 earnings Monday May 11 pre-market is the catalyst event — first report post-Calpine close. Risk skewed both ways: street looking for $8.21B revenue, ~$2.02B EBITDA, and any named hyperscaler datacenter customer. Stock down 13% YTD heading into print, so beats can fuel a rebound. Flagging as event risk, not a directional call.
CoreWeave shares up ~80% YTD vs S&P +7%; $99.4B backlog and S&P credit outlook upgrade to positive on Q1 print. Capex stepping up to $31-35B — watch for any signs the financing path strains margins.

CoreWeave raised FY26 capex by ~$10B (now $31-35B) on its May 7 print while Q2 revenue guide ($2.45-2.6B) came in light versus whispers. With backlog at $99.4B and revenue still scaling, the funding gap is the central debate - watch for incremental ABL/term-loan announcements or equity issuance signals before next print.
Q1 EPS $2.74 vs $2.59 est; revenue $11.12B vs ~$9B est; FY26 guide reaffirmed $11-$12; framework targets 20%+ EPS growth 2026-2029.
Q1 EPS $2.87 vs $1.32 est; EBITDA $1.49B; dividend hiked; FY26 EBITDA guide $6.8-$7.6B reaffirmed.
Shares +15% intraday to all-time high; market cap >$900B; UBS PT KRW 1.55M -> 1.7M citing HBM super-cycle. Risk: late-cycle entry.
HBM4 qualified by both Nvidia and AMD; full-scale supply June; 4nm HBM4 logic die pricing +40-50%. Catch-up vs SK Hynix accelerating.
Q1 net loss widened to $740M; capex guide raised to $31-$35B. Backlog +$32.6B QoQ to $99.4B but financing burden growing. Shares -11% on print.
FY26 guidance raised across all metrics; backlog at record EUR 146B; orders +29.5% YoY. Q2 full report May 12.
Apple preliminary foundry deal + Nvidia 'exciting products' tease lift sentiment; foundry thesis gaining customers.
Record €17.7B Q2 orders (book-to-bill 1.72); FY26 outlook raised across revenue, margin, net income and FCF; €6B buyback accelerated. Datacenter demand seen through 2030s.
Multiple sell-side hikes into May 20 print (Susquehanna $275, Goldman calling 'meaningfully undervalued'). Beat-and-raise expectations set high; CPI/macro setup adds two-way risk.
Q3 FY26 reports tomorrow after-close. Implied options move ~10%. Key prints: AI infra order pace vs $5B FY target, Silicon One G300 traction, Splunk attach.
MU up +30% over the past five sessions on AI memory shortage narrative; hot CPI print (3.8% YoY) and energy shock raise odds of multiple compression across high-beta AI names.

Down ~9% intraday on profit-taking and hot April CPI print (3.8% YoY); stock hit all-time high ~$113 on May 8 after +430% trailing-year gain and Apple foundry talks. Macro risk-off is triggering sector-wide reversal.

Down ~7-8% intraday ahead of Q1 2026 earnings release after the close; consensus expects a $0.19/share loss vs $0.07 year-ago. Stock had surged >30% since NRC Aurora PDC approval (May 6) and Nvidia partnership announcement; JPMorgan initiated Neutral at $83 (May 11). Investors watching cash burn and whether Switch/Equinix LOIs convert to binding contracts.

US MATCH Act (Multilateral Alignment of Technology Controls on Hardware) bill active in Congress would ban DUV lithography tool sales and service to China, extending existing EUV-only restrictions. China represents ~25-30% of ASML revenue; no vote date set but bill is live. Compounded by TSMC's delay of High-NA EUV adoption to 2029.

Controlling stockholders announced underwritten secondary offering of 20M Class A shares (company receives no proceeds); Barclays, Goldman Sachs, and JPMorgan book-running. Creates near-term dilution overhang despite RBC/UBS PT raises today and earlier hyperscaler master supply agreement wins.
Q1 print after-close May 12: EPS -$0.19 beat by 1c but stock -5.76% AH on widening loss; pipeline expanded to ~14 GW but contracts remain non-binding LOIs.
Three major desks (Wells Fargo $315, Susquehanna $275, Goldman beat-and-raise) all moved bullish into May 20 print. PT raises now 14-44% above current.
Q3 FY26 reports today after-close; options imply ~9.9% move. AI hyperscaler orders bogey $1B, bulls expect $1.5B+ given hyperscaler capex revisions.

Wolfspeed surged +24.65% after research firm Citrini named WOLF its single-stock AI infrastructure highlight, citing post-bankruptcy restructuring and SiC power electronics as critical for high-density AI datacenter power. Stock at ~$67, up ~180% YTD since debt restructuring.

ON Semiconductor +10.77% intraday as narrative shifts from EV-only story to high-voltage AI datacenter power infrastructure play. Continuation of B. Riley (Buy, $115 PT) and BofA upgrades from April. Stock trading $99-106 range.

Vertiv +8.45% continuing post-Q1 earnings rally: full-year 2026 outlook raised, liquid cooling and high-density AI datacenter power driving demand. Citigroup raised PT to $414, Evercore ISI raised PT to $425 from $350.

Constellation Energy -7.8% as Q1 2026 earnings revealed FY2026 EPS guidance midpoint $11.50 missed consensus $11.69. Net interest expense surged 73.3% YoY to $253M from $16.4B Calpine acquisition debt; cash fell from $3.64B to $0.8B; nuclear capacity factor declined to 92.3% from 94.1%.

Q3 FY26 record: rev $15.8B, EPS $1.06; FY26 AI infra orders target lifted to $9B from $5B; stock +15% pre-market on 9 PT raises (MS $120, GS $116, WF $130, PSC $132, UBS $132, BNP $132, JPM $120, BCS $121, BAC $114).

H200 export licenses cleared for ~10 Chinese tech firms (Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance, JD.com) up to 75k chips each as Trump-Xi summit opens. NVDA +3% overnight, into May 20 FY27 Q1 print. Risk: Beijing pressuring domestic buyers to defer.
SK Hynix touched KRW 2M intraday — first time ever; on cusp of $1T market cap. KOSPI200 concentration with Samsung now >50%.
Q2 FY26 reports after-bell tonight; consensus $2.68 EPS on $7.69B revenue. HBM equipment, advanced packaging, leading-edge logic in focus. Read-through for LRCX/KLAC/ASML.
Jane Street committing ~$6B for AI cloud capacity plus $1B equity at $109/share — quant validation of neocloud pricing power. Watch debt-exposure offloading at the banks as offsetting risk.

Q3 FY2026 blowout: revenue $15.84B (+12% YoY), adj. EPS $1.06, both beat; Q4 guide $16.7-16.9B vs $15.56B est. AI infrastructure orders $1.9B, tripled YoY. Stock +17-20% intraday — largest post-earnings move of the session.

HSBC downgraded to Reduce citing rally based on one-off deals, not fundamental improvement. UBS server share data: Intel at 54.9% (-370 bps QoQ), AMD gained 230 bps to 27.4%, ARM gained 140 bps to 17.7%. Foundry Q1 operating loss $2.3B; 18A/14A yield concerns persist. Stock -5.2% at open.

US Commerce Dept. cleared H200 chip sales to ~10 Chinese companies (Alibaba, Tencent, ByteDance, JD.com among recipients). Jensen Huang joined Trump-Xi summit delegation in Beijing. Huang estimates China AI market at $50B. Deliveries pending Beijing guidance. Stock +4% intraday.

Arista Networks +4.5% intraday on Cisco earnings read-through: CSCO AI networking orders tripled YoY to $1.9B, confirming hyperscaler networking capex cycle Arista participates in. Arista Q1 already beat with upward guidance revision.

Confirmed 18-day Samsung union strike starting May 21; HBM/foundry capacity at risk during peak AI memory qualification window. ~$700M/day cost estimate; industry impact estimates KRW 40T base, KRW 100T worst-case.
Record Q2 FY26: rev $7.91B vs $7.82B consensus, GAAP EPS $3.51 vs $2.71. Raised CY26 WFE growth to >30% from >20%. Q3 guide $8.95B vs $8.09B Street. Lynx Equity target to $540.
SK Hynix -7.66% Friday after briefly touching KRW 2M earlier in week. KOSPI -6.12% on foreign profit-taking on HBM winners. Memory-cycle bull thesis intact but positioning vulnerable.

Third sell-side rating cut in two weeks (Daiwa May 13 to Outperform; HSBC May 4 to Hold). Forward P/E ~33x 2027 EPS; insider selling $54.6M past 90 days; H2 gaming guide -20% on memory cost inflation.
China H200 export framework reportedly requires 25% revenue remittance to US Treasury; Beijing pressuring approved buyers to defer orders. Zero shipments executed despite licenses.

Q2 FY2026 record revenue $7.91B (consensus ~$7.7B), adj. EPS $2.86 vs consensus $2.68; Q3 guidance $8.95B (above estimates); chip equipment growth outlook raised to >30% YoY; stock +5% on five+ analyst PT raises.

Stock down ~5% May 15 as UBS data shows Intel server CPU market share fell 370 bps to 54.9% in Q1 2026 while AMD gained 230 bps (to 27.4%) and Arm gained 140 bps (to 17.7%). Broad foundry execution concerns persist; yield-driven sector weakness amplifies the move.
BTIG raises target to $621 (from $499) and RBC Capital raises to $650 (from $550) on May 14-15, citing platform consolidation momentum and expanding threat detection TAM.

Applied Materials down ~4.7% intraday May 17 after surging post-Q2 earnings (May 15), as April CPI 3.8% print eliminates 2026 Fed rate-cut expectations and pushes yields higher. Macro headwind compressing high-multiple semiconductor equipment names despite strong fundamentals; prior earnings analyst PT cluster at $500-$550 intact.

KLA Corp down ~4.5% intraday May 17 alongside semiconductor equipment peers (AMAT -4.7%, LRCX -4.1%) as April CPI 3.8% drives risk-off rotation out of high-multiple growth names. Brent crude elevated at ~$107 adds inflation persistence concern. No company-specific catalyst; macro driven.
NextEra $66.8B all-stock takeout at ~23% premium; +9.8% Monday. Arb spread will compress only after FERC + VA/NC/SC approvals (12-18mo).
Largest US power M&A ever — concentrates 110 GW + 51 GW contracted DC capacity. Multi-state PUC approval risk; -5.4% Monday on size/integration concern.
Court partially granted strike injunction (safety/wafer ops must run); 18-day strike still scheduled May 21 absent deal by May 19. HBM4 + foundry yields at risk.
Q1 FY27 Wednesday May 20 AMC — guide $78B +/- 2%, GM >74%. Blackwell mix + China commentary the print's swing factors.
-3% pre-market on Samsung strike spillover concerns; HBM/DDR cycle tightness a Q1 margin tailwind if strike proceeds.
-9% intraday on valuation reset; Q2 EPS guidance came in below consensus and sector-wide AI infrastructure multiple compression accelerated. Revenue and backlog growth remain intact.

CEO sold 200K shares (May 12) and CSO sold 374K shares (May 11). DA Davidson cut Buy -> Neutral, PT $175->$100, citing thin margins, debt-heavy structure, operating costs doubling YoY to $2.2B, and Q2 revenue guidance miss.
HSBC raises to $325 (from $295); Morgan Stanley to $285 (from $260) day-before print. Sell-side modeling Q1 rev $81B vs guide $78B; options imply 8-10% move on Wednesday.

Samsung-union mediation in final day Tuesday May 19; 18-day strike still set for May 21 absent agreement on 15%-of-OP bonus formula. PM called emergency meeting; estimated $700M/day strike cost. HBM4 production partly insulated by court injunction on wafer ops.
SK Hynix -5.16% to KRW 1,745,000 on broad Asia memory sell-off into NVDA print. Diversion-winner narrative still intact if Samsung strike proceeds Thursday.
30Y Treasury yield at 5.198% (19-yr high), Fed-hike probability for Dec at 48%. Long-duration neocloud financing economics deteriorating; rate-sensitive valuation under pressure.

Rate-sensitive renewables/fuel-cell complex hit hard by yield spike (FSLR -4.4%, ENPH -5.8%, GNRC -4.8% Tuesday); read-through to Bloom. Barclays PT $254 equal-weight is the most recent rating (May 12).
BofA reinstates at Underperform ($160 target) citing structural AI-driven reset: agents and cheaper competitors eroding CRM platform lock-in; thesis-level bear case from major institution.

CoreWeave launches HPC infrastructure-backed loan facility, adding to already stretched balance sheet: ~1% EBIT margins, 9%+ borrowing costs on existing debt, and now new collateralized facility — triple-leverage structure ahead of rate-sensitive environment.
Q1 FY27 print today AMC; consensus $78B rev / $1.77 EPS / $73B datacenter; Street PTs $285-$325 cluster; options price 8% implied move.

47,000-worker 18-day strike begins Thursday May 21 through June 7 after mediation collapse; up to 12% quarterly operating-profit hit (JPM); HBM4 partially insulated by injunction but R&D/node migration exposed.
Memory complex repricing post Samsung strike confirmation — SK Hynix gains foundry/memory share if strike runs full 18 days; HBM4 deliveries to NVDA Rubin remain on critical path.
UBS top-European-pick PT EUR 1,900; Morgan Stanley raises 40% to EUR 1,400; TSMC 2026 capex hike pulls through EUV/High-NA orders.
Memory supercycle reinforced — Hood cited MU as marginal memory cost driver in MSFT $190B capex; Samsung strike disruption is incremental tailwind for MU/Hynix share.
Q1 FY26 blowout: revenue 0.25B (+38% YoY), datacenter segment .8B (+57% YoY). Q2 guide raised to 1.2B vs 0.7B consensus. Stock +8% intraday.
Stock +6.7% after Bloomberg reported Intel held preliminary acquisition talks with AI chip startup Tenstorrent (RISC-V architecture, valuation >B). No deal announced.
BofA reinstated Underperform with $160 PT. Stock near 52-week lows, -35% YTD. Analysts cite sluggish new-customer growth, weak pricing power, and limited Agentforce AI monetization traction.
Q1 FY27: $81.6B rev (+85% YoY, beat $78.9B), datacenter $75.2B (+92%), Q2 guide $91B vs $86.8B, $80B buyback, dividend 25x to $0.25
Day 1 of 18-day strike (May 21–June 7): 45,000+ workers off; JPM models $14-20.8B OP hit, ~$700M/day in sales
Share-gain bid as Samsung strike begins: 3-4% of global DRAM at risk; Jefferies sees order migration
Memory share-gain beneficiary from Samsung strike; MSFT Q1 call flagged MU as marginal memory-cost driver in $190B capex
Q1 datacenter electrification orders ($2.4B) exceed all of 2025; 21 GW new gas-turbine agreements; lead times to 7 years

Q1 FY2027: Revenue $81.6B (beat $79.2B est.), EPS $1.87 (beat $1.76 est.), Q2 guide $91B (above $86.8B est.), $80B buyback announced. Stock declined ~2% at open — fourth consecutive post-earnings drop despite a beat-and-raise. Bulge-bracket PT raises (RBC, Wedbush, MS, GS) insufficient to hold price. Expectations fully priced; watch for rotation.

Applied Digital announced a $18.2B long-term lease for its Polaris Forge 3 AI campus (stock +16% today). Largest AI infrastructure lease on record signals sustained hyperscaler demand for colocation and wholesale datacenter capacity — positive read-through for Digital Realty and the broader datacenter REIT thesis.

Applied Digital $18.2B AI campus megadeal (announced May 21) underscores accelerating hyperscaler datacenter buildout pace. Positive demand signal for datacenter power and thermal management infrastructure suppliers.
Strike averted via 10.5% profit-share deal; shares +7.6% in Seoul; HBM lines stay full-staffed into Q3.
Samsung supply-disruption tailwind that fueled rally now removed; recent BofA $950 PT predicated partly on share-gain optionality that's now weaker.
Apple 18A foundry talks reportedly preliminary-agreed per WSJ/Yahoo. Tier-1 external customer would re-rate the foundry segment; awaiting volume/process confirmation.
DDTL 5.0 rated Ba2/BB+; first publicly syndicated HPC infrastructure-backed financing. Demonstrates that GPU-collateralized debt now has institutional bid depth.

+15% intraday surge on six coordinated analyst price-target raises (Mizuho $300, JPMorgan $280, Citi $290, BofA $280, Wells Fargo $270, Morgan Stanley $170); $43B AI server backlog exiting FY2026 with FY2027 revenue guide ~$50B (+100% YoY); Q1 FY27 earnings due May 28 is next near-term catalyst.

+9% intraday sector read-through from Dell's AI server momentum; no independent catalyst but coordinated analyst upgrade wave on Dell lifted all AI server hardware names; HPE reports its own AI infrastructure traction in upcoming earnings.

+5% intraday sector rally lift alongside Dell and HPE; company-specific governance review and export-control scrutiny are limiting outperformance relative to peers; AI server demand tailwind is real but execution risk remains elevated.

+5.4% today on continued AI datacenter electrification sentiment; no single confirmed intraday catalyst; power demand narrative sustains as hyperscaler capital expenditure guidance (MSFT, AMZN, META) remains at record levels through 2027.

Wolfspeed surged ~175% from late-April (~$25) to $71.74 by May 21 on Citrini Research bullish note (May 13) calling WOLF the SiC power-chip winner for AI datacenter power density. Stock +20% pre-market May 13. Fundamentals remain challenged: -27% GAAP gross margin, negative operating income, recently emerged from bankruptcy. Speculative momentum only — no earnings inflection confirmed.

Synopsys +5.26% on May 22 ahead of Q2 FY2026 earnings scheduled May 27 after close; options pricing ~8% implied move. Catalyst: $125M semiconductor research consortium announced at UCLA (Synopsys, Meta, Broadcom, Applied Materials, GlobalFoundries). Consensus: 15 Strong Buy / 4 Hold; avg target ~$480. Key watch: EDA licensing trends and AI-chip design tool adoption.

Constellation Energy sold off ~12.6% post-Q1 2026 earnings despite a strong beat (revenue +64% YoY to $11.12B, EPS $2.74 vs $2.54 est.) and reaffirmed 2026 adj. EPS guidance of $11-12/share. FCF outlook of $8.4B over 2026-2027, expanding to $11.5-13B in 2028-2029 with 20%+ earnings growth guided through 2029. Selloff appears driven by completion of buyback and elevated pre-earnings expectations, not fundamentals — potential re-entry level.
Quarterly order intake reportedly exceeded all of last year's; multi-year backlog and written guidance raises support continued AI-power order momentum.

Divergent analyst views ahead of June 3 Q2 FY2027 earnings: DZ Bank downgraded to Sell (target $500) on valuation stretch and July 2024 outage overhang, while KeyBanc ($700), Barclays ($650), and Stifel ($660) all raised targets on Mythos conference demand signals and channel-check bullishness. $200 spread between bear and bull targets indicates high uncertainty.

SK Hynix broke above 2,000,000 won for the first time (+6.85% on May 26), leading a Korea/Taiwan/Japan chip rally on AI memory demand; concentration risk rising as Samsung+SK Hynix hit ~42% of the KOSPI.

Stock surged 18% to $886.60 on May 26, crossing $1 trillion market cap for the first time, after UBS tripled its price target to a Street-high $1,625 (from $535). UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri argues AI has permanently reshaped memory market fundamentals: Micron HBM supply is sold out for all of 2026, customers are shifting to multi-year supply agreements, and UBS raised CY2027-29 EPS estimates to $155/$167/$117.

DOE selected Oklo to negotiate entry into the Surplus Plutonium Utilization Program on May 26, enabling conversion of Cold War-era weapons-grade plutonium into advanced reactor fuel. This de-risks Oklo fuel supply constraints and builds on the October 2025 newcleo partnership (up to $2 billion potential investment). Stock rose 10.4% to $72.72. Texas Capital reaffirmed Buy / $93 on the catalyst.
HA turbine fleet tops 4M operating hours; +5.3% on May 26 amid record Q1 datacenter electrification orders ($2.4B, > all of 2025). Turbine slots tight through 2030.
Memory supercycle: Counterpoint sees DRAM/HBM prices +~40% through Q2 2026; SK Hynix flags sustained HBM upcycle. Structural AI-driven demand tailwind.

Cybersecurity sector pressure: peer Zscaler (ZS) sold off ~23% on May 27 after Q3 earnings showed strong revenue growth (+25% YoY) but FCF margin guidance cut due to elevated AI infrastructure capex. Investor caution on margin compression from AI spending is a read-through for CRWD valuation multiples.
Q1 FY27 product revenue +34% YoY to $1.33B (record sequential growth); FY27 guide raised to $5.84B; NRR 126%; $6B five-year AWS pact. Shares +~37% premarket with desk targets lifted to $272–$325.

Q2 FY2026 beat: EPS $3.35 vs $3.15 est, revenue $2.28B vs $2.25B est, full-year guidance raised. Stock dropped ~8% on sell-the-news reaction — narrow guidance raise and restructuring cost concerns weighed. Largest S&P 500 decliner on May 28 despite the beat.

Q1 FY2026 revenue $163.5M (+22% YoY, beat consensus by 17%) with sharply improving gross margins. CEO reiterated EBITDAS-positive target for Q4 2026; $275M expected from hydrogen asset monetization including $142M Stream Data Centers deal closing June 2026. Stock +~6%.

Record Q1 FY27: revenue $43.8B (+88%), EPS $4.86 (+214%), AI server revenue +757% to $16.1B, $24.4B AI orders, $51.3B backlog; FY27 guide raised to ~$167B midpoint and AI-server target to $60B; plus $9.7B Pentagon-Microsoft deal.

Q1 FY2027: revenue $43.8B (+88% YoY, beat by 23%), EPS $4.86 vs $2.96 est. AI server revenue $16.1B (+757% YoY), $24.4B in new AI orders. FY2027 guide raised to ~$60B AI server revenue. Stock +33% intraday.
Q1 FY2027: product revenue $1.33B (+34% YoY), RPO $4.8B (+28%). EPS $0.39 vs $0.14 est. Announced $6B multi-year AWS commitment. Stock +36%, best single-day gain in company history.
Sympathy rally +16% driven by Dell Q1 FY2027 AI server blowout (+757% YoY). Validates hyperscaler capex acceleration thesis directly benefiting Super Micro's server addressable market.

+8.75% intraday. Dell Q1 FY2027 blowout validated the Dell AI Factory with NVIDIA partnership, which integrates Palantir Foundry and AIP. Enterprise AI spending acceleration reinforces Palantir's US commercial pipeline.

+14.05% intraday. New multi-year Experian global partnership integrating data into ServiceNow AI Platform for agentic workflows; expanded Wipro AI partnership. Snowflake earnings inverted SaaSpocalypse narrative, triggering broad enterprise software re-rating.
+6.7% intraday as Snowflake Q1 FY2027 results reframed AI as a consumption driver for enterprise data platforms, not a replacement. Re-rates Oracle alongside broader enterprise software sector.

HSBC upgraded Hold to Buy, raising PT from $176 to $289 (64% implied upside). Third sell-side upgrade in five trading days. Q1 FY27 product revenue +34% YoY and accelerating consumption model converging analyst sentiment toward re-rating.
Q1 2026: revenue $10.25B (+38% YoY), beat consensus; FY guidance raised; new 1.6TbE AI networking switches announced. Stock +8% intraday.

RTX Spark launches NVIDIA directly into premium Windows PC silicon — a market Intel has dominated for decades. Pre-market -6.5%, sympathy losers AMD/Qualcomm. Thesis risk: structural share loss in client compute on top of fab struggles, with no credible counter until 18A-class products ship.

GTC Taipei + RTX Spark + Spectrum-X annual cadence broadens NVIDIA's TAM into client compute and networking on top of existing datacenter dominance. Pre-market +2.1%, with Broadcom earnings on Jun 3 as the next AI infrastructure read-through.

Samsung Electronics +10% to all-time high on AI memory pricing strength; Kospi closed +3.68%. HBM/AI DRAM pricing forecast up to +63% this quarter. Watch for follow-through in SK Hynix and Micron sympathy.

Up ~5% on Computex 2026 keynote: Jensen Huang confirmed Vera Rubin chips in full production with first systems shipping this fall (Anthropic, OpenAI, Dell, Oracle among first customers); also unveiled RTX Spark, an Arm-based laptop/PC chip co-developed with Microsoft marking expansion into consumer compute market.

Q1 FY2027 earnings crushed estimates: revenue 3.8B (+88% YoY) vs consensus 5.4B; AI server revenue 6.1B (+757% YoY); 1.3B AI order backlog; FY27 guidance raised to 67B midpoint. Stock up 6% today. Multiple analysts doubled price targets.
Q2 FY26 revenue $10.68B (+40%) vs $9.79B est, EPS $0.79 vs $0.54. Networking (Juniper) +148%, Cloud & AI +23%. FY26 EPS guide raised to $3.35-$3.45 from $2.30-$2.50. Shares +27-30% AH.
Intra-quarter datacenter guidance raise to ~$1B 2026 revenue (prior >$500M) and possible doubling in 2027 on AWS multi-year deal. +8.2% in Paris.
SK Hynix chair: double memory wafer capacity in 5 years, AI shortage through 2030, 2026 capex > KRW 30.2T, US ADR filing targeting June-July listing window ($10-14B raise).
Morgan Stanley OW, PT $485 (from $470). Models AI rev $10.8B Q2 -> $16.9B Q3, $120B by 2027. +6.1% pre-market into Wed AMC earnings.
